_comment: Branchless equality compare with constant: (A == N) → A (0xFF=true, 0x00=false)
_principle: CY(CP N+1) XOR CY(CP N) = 1 iff A==N. Uses: (A<=N) XOR (A<N) = (A==N)
_not_contradicts: GPU proof that Z→A branchless is impossible for ARBITRARY Z — this requires KNOWN CONSTANT (two CPs with N and N+1)

general.notes: Saves original A in B. Two CY materializations XOR'd. B and C clobbered.

n_eq_0.notes: SUB 1 sets CY=1 iff A<1 i.e. A==0. SBC A,A materializes. Only A,F clobbered.

n_eq_255.notes: CP 255 sets CY=0 iff A==255. SBC+CPL inverts.

a_neq_0.notes: NEG sets CY=1 when A!=0 (result is non-zero). Inverse of n_eq_0.

timing_comparison.when_branchless_wins: When result needed as register value for masking/blending, not for control flow

applications: Inline computation without jump (e.g., inside expression evaluation)
Switch/table: combine multiple eq_compare results with OR to build case bitmap
Video codec: check if pixel value equals threshold constant
